item_a.name: Lenovo Yoga 9i 2-in-1 Laptop
item_a.image_alt: Lenovo Yoga 9i convertible laptop in tent mode with active stylus
pros: Superior battery life up to 14 hours
Excellent keyboard and trackpad for productivity
Rotating soundbar hinge with Bowers & Wilkins speakers
Includes active stylus pen in the box
cons: Heavier at 3.09 lbs making it less portable
Longer to resume from sleep mode
item_a.best_for: Content creators and professionals who prioritize a traditional laptop experience with convertible flexibility and premium audio.

item_b.name: Microsoft Surface Pro 9
item_b.image_alt: Microsoft Surface Pro 9 tablet with detachable keyboard and Surface Slim Pen
pros: Lightweight tablet design at 1.94 lbs without keyboard
Detachable keyboard offers tablet and laptop modes
cons: Keyboard and pen sold separately adding $280 to cost
Less stable on lap due to kickstand design
item_b.best_for: Mobile professionals and digital artists who need true tablet portability with laptop capabilities when docked.

comparison_summary: The Yoga 9i gives you a complete premium laptop with fantastic battery life and everything in the box. The Surface Pro 9 trades convenience for genuine tablet portability. Think about whether you need a laptop that sometimes works as a tablet, or a tablet that sometimes works as a laptop.
verdict_text: The Yoga 9i is the better laptop with superior value, while the Surface Pro 9 is the better tablet—pick based on which mode you'll actually use most.

key_differences.item_b: 1.94 lbs tablet only (3.5 lbs with keyboard)

key_differences.item_a: Rotating soundbar with Bowers & Wilkins speakers
Full-size backlit keyboard with 1.3mm key travel
key_differences.item_b: Detachable Type Cover with 1.1mm key travel
Intel Core i5/i7 12th gen or Microsoft SQ3 ARM

seo_content:
I've spent time with both the Lenovo Yoga 9i and Surface Pro 9, and they represent two completely different philosophies about what a convertible computer should be.

The Yoga 9i is a traditional 2-in-1 laptop—a premium clamshell that flips around 360 degrees through a special hinge. The Surface Pro 9 approaches things differently: it's fundamentally a tablet that becomes a laptop when you attach the optional keyboard cover.

That detachable design is the Surface Pro's main appeal. Without the keyboard, it weighs just 1.94 pounds, making it genuinely comfortable to hold like an iPad. If you're a digital artist or someone who annotates documents all day with a stylus, this matters. But here's the catch—the keyboard and pen are sold separately, adding $280 to your total cost. And that kickstand design? It's awkward on your lap and basically unusable on airplane tray tables.

The Yoga 9i weighs 3.09 pounds, so it's heavier than the Surface Pro with keyboard attached. But what you get in return is worth considering. Battery life hits 14 hours versus the Surface Pro's 10-11 hours. The keyboard has 1.3mm of key travel, which makes typing far more comfortable during long work sessions. The audio quality is exceptional—there's a rotating soundbar hinge with Bowers & Wilkins speakers built in. I've tested dozens of laptops, and the Yoga's audio genuinely impressed me. For video conferences or media work, it makes a real difference.

Let's talk about value. The Yoga 9i includes an active stylus in the box. Surface Pro buyers need to budget an additional $150 for the Surface Slim Pen. Configure both with 16GB RAM and 512GB storage, and the Yoga typically costs less overall. The Surface Pro does offer more configuration options, including 5G connectivity if you need it.

Both displays are excellent. The Yoga's 14-inch OLED panel delivers deeper blacks. The Surface Pro's PixelSense screen packs more pixels into a smaller 13-inch space. Both handle stylus input well with low latency, though the Surface Pro's detachable form factor feels more natural for extended drawing sessions.

Your decision really depends on how you work. Pick the Surface Pro 9 if you genuinely need tablet portability, spend significant time in tablet mode, or need that optional 5G. Choose the Yoga 9i if battery life matters, you value audio quality, you want a great typing experience, or you'd rather not pay extra for accessories that should be included.

faq.q: Can the Lenovo Yoga be used as a tablet like the Surface Pro?
faq.a: Yes, but it's not the same experience. The Yoga folds 360 degrees into tablet mode, but at 3.09 lbs it's pretty heavy to hold for long periods. The Surface Pro actually detaches from its keyboard, giving you a much lighter 1.94 lb tablet that's comfortable for extended handheld use.

faq.q: Which has better battery life, Lenovo Yoga or Surface Pro?
faq.a: The Yoga 9i wins here by a decent margin—up to 14 hours versus the Surface Pro 9's 10-11 hours. If you need to work all day without hunting for outlets, the Yoga is your better option.

faq.q: Do I need to buy a separate keyboard for the Surface Pro?
faq.a: Yes, and this adds up fast. The Type Cover keyboard runs about $130 separately, and the Surface Slim Pen is another $150. The Yoga 9i includes both the keyboard and an active stylus right in the box at the base price.

faq.q: Which is better for drawing and digital art?
faq.a: Most artists prefer the Surface Pro 9 because it's lighter as a standalone tablet, the kickstand creates a more natural drawing angle, and Microsoft's pen technology is really well optimized. That said, both devices handle stylus input with low latency, so the Yoga can certainly get the job done.

faq.q: Can both devices run the same Windows software?
faq.a: Mostly yes, but watch out for the 5G Surface Pro model. Most Surface Pro 9 units use Intel processors and run everything. But the 5G version has Microsoft's ARM-based SQ3 processor, which struggles with some older x86 applications. All Yoga 9i models use standard Intel processors with full compatibility.

faq.q: Is Lenovo Yoga 9i 2-in-1 Laptop better than Microsoft Surface Pro 9?
faq.a: It depends on your primary use case. The Yoga 9i is the better laptop with superior value, excellent battery life, and everything included, while the Surface Pro 9 is the better tablet with genuine portability. Choose the Yoga 9i if you need a laptop first, or the Surface Pro 9 if you need a tablet first.

faq.q: Should I buy Lenovo Yoga 9i 2-in-1 Laptop or Microsoft Surface Pro 9?
faq.a: Buy the Yoga 9i if you want a premium laptop that occasionally doubles as a tablet—it offers better overall value and battery life. Buy the Surface Pro 9 if you're primarily a tablet user who occasionally needs laptop capabilities, as it prioritizes genuine tablet portability over laptop convenience.

faq.q: What is the difference between Lenovo Yoga 9i 2-in-1 Laptop and Microsoft Surface Pro 9?
faq.a: The Yoga 9i is a full-featured laptop with a 2-in-1 design, offering better battery life, more included accessories, and superior laptop performance. The Surface Pro 9 is a tablet-first device that works as a laptop with a detachable keyboard, prioritizing portability and tablet functionality over traditional laptop convenience.

meta_title: Lenovo Yoga vs Surface Pro (2026): Laptop or Tablet First?
meta_description: Detailed comparison of Lenovo Yoga 9i vs Microsoft Surface Pro 9. We break down portability, battery life, value, and convertible design to help you...
comparison_intro: Choosing between the Lenovo Yoga 9i 2-in-1 Laptop or Microsoft Surface Pro 9 comes down to understanding what you'll actually use most—and the difference between Lenovo Yoga 9i 2-in-1 Laptop and Microsoft Surface Pro 9 is more about design philosophy than raw power. When comparing the Lenovo Yoga 9i 2-in-1 Laptop compared to Microsoft Surface Pro 9, you're really asking which is better for your lifestyle: a laptop that doubles as a tablet, or a tablet that doubles as a laptop. In our Lenovo Yoga 9i 2-in-1 Laptop vs Microsoft Surface Pro 9 matchup, we'll show you exactly where each device shines and help you determine which one deserves a spot on your desk.
